text: Good afternoon and thank you for joining Apple's quarterly earnings call. We're thrilled to report record revenue of $89.5 billion, up 5 percent year over year. iPhone revenue was $43.8 billion with strong growth in emerging markets. Our services business reached an all-time high of $22.3 billion. Looking ahead, we see continued momentum in AI and spatial computing. We're investing heavily in Apple Intelligence features rolling out next quarter. Gross margins expanded to 45.2 percent. We returned over $27 billion to shareholders this quarter through dividends and buybacks. Now I'll hand it over to Luca for the detailed financial breakdown.

Welcome to JPMorgan Chase's third quarter earnings call. Net income was $12.9 billion, driven by strong performance across all business segments. Investment banking fees increased 31 percent year over year. We're seeing healthy loan growth in commercial banking and card services. Credit quality remains strong with net charge-offs at historical lows. However, we remain cautious about the macroeconomic environment. Inflation and geopolitical tensions present uncertainty. We've increased our loan loss reserves by $300 million as a precautionary measure. Looking at 2024, we expect net interest income to moderate as rate cuts begin. Our capital position is robust with a CET1 ratio of 14.3 percent.

Thank you for joining Microsoft's fiscal year 2024 second quarter earnings conference call. Revenue was $62 billion, up 18 percent year over year. Azure cloud revenue grew 29 percent, exceeding our guidance. AI services contributed 6 percentage points of that growth. Copilot adoption is accelerating across Office 365, GitHub, and Dynamics. We're seeing enterprises move from AI experimentation to production deployment at scale. Operating margins expanded to 48 percent. We continue to invest aggressively in data center capacity to meet growing AI demand. Our commercial remaining performance obligation reached $210 billion. Looking forward, we expect revenue of $64 to $65 billion next quarter.
